yaml_syntax_rules.rule: DATE_TRUNC requires a single-quoted interval string
yaml_syntax_rules.note: The interval keyword MUST be single-quoted inside DATE_TRUNC.

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: TIMESTAMPADD / TIMESTAMPDIFF use bare unquoted singular unit keywords
yaml_syntax_rules.note: Unit is a bare keyword (MONTH, DAY, HOUR, MINUTE, SECOND), singular, no quotes.
Use EXTRACT(unit FROM col), not DATE_PART.

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: DATEDIFF takes exactly two arguments and returns days
yaml_syntax_rules.note: For other units use TIMESTAMPDIFF(MINUTE, start, end).

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: All string literals must be single-quoted everywhere in expressions
yaml_syntax_rules.note: Applies to comparisons, CASE THEN/ELSE results, IN lists, CONCAT separators, LIKE patterns.

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: CASE WHEN results that contain special characters must be single-quoted
yaml_syntax_rules.note: Every THEN and ELSE value that is a string literal needs quotes.

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: FILTER clause syntax uses parenthesized WHERE
Join 'on' clause uses source.col = alias.col format
yaml_syntax_rules.note: The root table is always 'source'; joined tables use their 'name' alias. Never use fully qualified names in join on clauses.

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: Dimension/measure expressions referencing joined table columns use alias.col
yaml_syntax_rules.note: Use the short join name (alias), not the fully qualified table name.

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: Version must be string '1.1' not number 1.1
CONCAT separator arguments must be single-quoted string literals
yaml_syntax_rules.note: Any non-column, non-function argument in CONCAT must be a quoted string. -Q is parsed as 'subtract column Q' without quotes.

yaml_syntax_rules.rule: Window measures use a nested 'window' object with order_by and range/rows
yaml_syntax_rules.wrong: {"name": "30-Day Rolling Avg Revenue", "expr": "AVG(SUM(total_amount)) OVER (ORDER BY order_date RANGE INTERVAL 30 DAYS PRECEDING)"}
yaml_syntax_rules.note: Window logic goes in the 'window' sub-object, NOT in the expr string. The expr contains the aggregate function only.

measure_patterns.description: Templates for non-trivial measures. Replace placeholders (col, cond, entity_col, etc.) with actual column names from the metadata.

rate.template: SUM(CASE WHEN {condition} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)

examples.name: Fulfillment Rate
examples.expr: SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'fulfilled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)

examples.name: Readmission Rate
examples.expr: SUM(CASE WHEN is_readmission = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)
examples.comment: Fraction of encounters that are readmissions

examples.name: Conversion Rate
examples.expr: SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'converted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)

examples.name: Orders per Account
examples.comment: Average number of orders per account

percentage.template: ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN {condition} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1)

examples.name: Return Pct
examples.expr: ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN is_returned = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1)

examples.name: Overdue Pct
examples.expr: ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN due_date < current_date() AND status != 'paid' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1)

examples.name: Active Customers
examples.expr: COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FILTER (WHERE last_order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', current_date()))

examples.name: Repeat Patients
examples.comment: Patients with more than one visit

examples.name: Avg Wait Time
examples.comment: Average minutes from arrival to being seen

window_rolling.template: AVG(SUM({col})) with window: {order_by: date_col, range: INTERVAL N DAYS PRECEDING}

examples.name: 7-Day Rolling Order Count
examples.comment: Rolling 7-day average of daily order counts

window_cumulative.template: SUM(SUM({col})) with window: {order_by: date_col, rows: UNBOUNDED PRECEDING}

join_templates.description: Correct join format for Unity Catalog metric views. The root table is always referenced as 'source'; joined tables are referenced by their short 'name' alias.

star_schema.description: Fact table joins one or more dimension tables

example.note: Dimensions from joined tables use alias.column format (e.g. customers.segment). The fact table columns use bare names (e.g. total_amount).

snowflake.description: Nested joins where a dimension table joins another dimension (DBR 17.1+)

common_mistakes: Using fully qualified names in 'on' clause: on: catalog.schema.orders.customer_id = catalog.schema.customers.id -- WRONG
Using 'using' instead of 'on' for non-trivial joins -- 'using' only works for same-named columns
Forgetting to reference joined columns with alias prefix in dimensions/measures
Using the same alias name as a column name in the source table

anti_patterns: NEVER produce a metric view whose only measure is COUNT(*) or COUNT(1). Always add at least one ratio, rate, or filtered aggregate.
NEVER use unquoted string literals in any SQL expression. Every string value must be single-quoted.
NEVER invent column names. Only reference columns that exist in the metadata context provided.
NEVER create duplicate metric views for the same analytical theme. Consolidate related measures into one view.
NEVER use plural date units (MONTHS, HOURS) or quoted units in TIMESTAMPADD/TIMESTAMPDIFF.
NEVER omit joins when FK relationships exist and the questions ask for breakdowns by attributes in another table.
NEVER use bare column names in joined table references. Always qualify with the join alias (customers.segment, not segment when it comes from a joined table).
NEVER prefix dimension names with the join alias in the display name. Use 'Industry' not 'account.Industry'.
NEVER use DATE_PART -- use EXTRACT(unit FROM col) instead.
NEVER return a metric view with zero dimensions or zero measures.
validation_checklist: Every string literal in every expression is single-quoted (comparisons, CASE results, IN lists, LIKE patterns, CONCAT separators)
Every column reference exists in the provided metadata (source table or joined table)
DATE_TRUNC uses quoted interval: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', col)
TIMESTAMPADD/TIMESTAMPDIFF use bare singular unit: TIMESTAMPADD(MONTH, 1, col)
At least one non-trivial measure exists (ratio, rate, filtered aggregate, or conditional count -- not just COUNT(*) and SUM)
Joins are included when FK relationships exist AND questions reference attributes from related tables
Join 'on' clauses use source.col = alias.col format (not fully qualified names)
Dimensions from joined tables use alias.col format in their expr
Every measure and dimension has a descriptive 'comment' field
Metric view 'name' is unique and theme-based (not just the table name)
The 'source' field is a fully qualified three-part name (catalog.schema.table)
FILTER clauses use the (WHERE ...) syntax with parentheses
